data.title: Pikachu Illustrator PSA 10 sets record at Goldin sale
data.description:
Logan Paul, Pikachu Illustrator PSA 10, most expensive trading card — Goldin auction verified by Guinness and PSA; rarity and provenance drove liquidity event.

data.title: BHP Group Reports Earnings Boost Amid Copper Price Surge
data.description: BHP Group has reported a significant increase in earnings for the six months ending in December, driven by a substantial rise in copper prices. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite this positive development, the company faced challenges due to stagnant demand in China, which affected its iron ore and steelmaking coal sectors. The surge in copper prices provided a notable boost to BHP's financial performance, underscoring the commodity's importance to the company's portfolio. However, the plateauing demand in China remains a concern for its other business areas.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,000 USDT with a 2.21% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 16, 2026, 21:59 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,002.670044 USDT, with a narrowed 2.21% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Expands Influence in Africa Through Church Expansion
data.description: Russia has leveraged the expansion of its national church to enhance its influence across Africa. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how this strategic move has become a significant asset for the Russian state. The church's growth in the region is seen as a tool for extending Russia's geopolitical reach, aligning with broader efforts to strengthen ties with African nations. This development underscores Russia's ongoing strategy to increase its presence and sway in global affairs, particularly in regions where it seeks to counter Western influence.

data.title: CME Predicts Fed Rate Decisions for Upcoming Months
data.description: The CME's FedWatch tool has released its latest predictions regarding the Federal Reserve's interest rate decisions. According to Jin10, the probability of the Fed cutting rates by 25 basis points in March stands at 7.8%, while the likelihood of maintaining the current rate is 92.2%. Looking ahead to April, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut increases to 25.3%, with a 73.1% chance of rates remaining unchanged and a 1.6% chance of a cumulative 50 basis point cut. By June, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut rises to 52.6%.

data.title: Binance clarifies listing roles as He Yi says Chase is BD
data.description:
He Yi clarifies Chase handles BD, not listings; Binance separates sourcing from approval. It explains the Binance listing process and what projects can expect.
source: https://coincu.com/news/binance-clarifies-listing-roles-as-he-yi-says-chase-is-bd/

data.title: Dollar Index Rises as Euro and Pound Decline Against U.S. Dollar
data.description: The U.S. dollar showed stability as the dollar index climbed back above the 97 mark in New York's late trading session, closing at 97.07, marking an increase of approximately 0.2%. According to RTHK, the euro fell by 0.1% against the dollar, with earlier reports showing it at $1.1853. Meanwhile, the British pound declined by about 0.2%, trading at $1.3626. The dollar also rose against the Japanese yen, increasing by 0.5% to 153.5 yen.

data.title: Asia Struggles to Secure Climate Adaptation Funding
data.description: Asia has been significantly impacted by climate change, yet it continues to face challenges in attracting necessary adaptation funding. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the region's difficulties in securing financial support to address climate-related issues. Despite the urgent need for resources to combat the effects of climate change, Asia's efforts to obtain funding have not been successful. The lack of adaptation funding poses a threat to the region's ability to effectively manage and mitigate the consequences of climate change.

data.title: Norwegian Skier Atle Lie McGrath's Disappointment After Slalom Loss
data.description: Norwegian skier Atle Lie McGrath experienced a heartbreaking moment after missing out on a slalom gold medal that seemed within his grasp. New York Times posted on X, McGrath, visibly upset, discarded his poles, removed his skis, and walked away, reflecting on the loss as the world observed his reaction. The incident highlights the emotional intensity and pressure athletes face in competitive sports.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | BHP Secures $4.3 Billion Prepayment in Silver Streaming Deal
data.description: BHP has announced a long-term streaming agreement with Wheaton Precious Metals International Ltd. According to Jin10, the agreement stipulates that BHP will receive a prepayment of $4.3 billion upon the completion of the transaction. BHP will deliver silver to Wheaton based on its share of silver production from the Antamina mine.

data.title: Sims Predicts Strong Non-Ferrous Metals Market Amid Tariff Support
data.description: Sims, a global leader in metal and electronics recycling, anticipates that the non-ferrous metals market will remain robust. According to Jin10, the company expects tariffs to continue supporting demand for both ferrous and non-ferrous metals in the United States. This outlook suggests sustained interest and activity in the metals sector, driven by ongoing trade policies.

data.title: Brazilian Carnival Parade Sparks Debate Over Political Campaigning
data.description: A Carnival parade in Brazil dedicated to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has stirred controversy, with opposition leaders claiming it served as an inappropriate campaign event for the leftist leader. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the debate surrounding the parade's political implications. Critics argue that the event, which celebrated Lula's presidency, blurred the lines between cultural celebration and political endorsement. The parade's organizers, however, maintain that the tribute was purely cultural, aiming to honor Lula's contributions to Brazil. The incident has sparked discussions on the role of public events in political campaigning, as Brazil approaches its next election cycle.

data.title: Chile's Cherry Exports to China Face Challenges
data.description: Chile has experienced a second consecutive season of underwhelming cherry sales in China. Bloomberg posted on X that the country exported an excessive amount of cherries too early, ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday, a period when demand typically reaches its highest point. This timing issue has impacted the overall success of Chile's cherry exports in the Chinese market.

data.title: Dutch State Secretary Resigns Amid CV Controversy
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X. The Dutch State Secretary, who was set to oversee the implementation of a 36% unrealized gains tax, has resigned following revelations about inaccuracies in her curriculum vitae. It was discovered that she had only completed high school, contrary to claims of higher educational achievements. Her resignation comes amid growing scrutiny and criticism over her qualifications for the role. The controversy has sparked discussions about the vetting process for public officials in the Netherlands.

data.title: Elana Meyers Taylor Achieves Olympic Gold in Bobsledding
data.description: Elana Meyers Taylor, a prominent figure in bobsledding, has secured an Olympic gold medal, marking a significant milestone in her illustrious career. New York Times posted on X, highlighting her achievement at the age of 41. Meyers Taylor, who is also a mother to two young deaf sons, has been celebrated for her contributions to the sport and her perseverance in balancing her professional and personal life. Her victory adds to her legacy as one of the all-time greats in bobsledding.

data.title: Eileen Gu Secures Silver in Freeski Big Air as Megan Oldham Takes Gold
data.description: Eileen Gu, the defending champion in the freeski big air event, claimed the silver medal after a remarkable performance by Canada's Megan Oldham secured her the gold. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the event's outcome. Italy's Flora Tabanelli, competing in her first Olympics despite an ACL injury, earned the bronze medal. The competition showcased exceptional talent and determination among the athletes.

data.title: Hayden Davis of LIBRA token fame returns as a meme trader
data.description:
Hayden Davis, known for launching the LIBRA token, is back to trading memes. This time, he tested the market with smaller trending tokens, but lost $3M.

data.title: Mega-Cap Tech Stocks Fall Below 50-Day Moving Averages
data.description:
All eight major technology and tech-related stocks have dropped below their 50-day moving averages. Bespoke Investment Group posted on X, highlighting this trend among the leading companies in the sector. This development indicates a potential shift in market sentiment towards these stocks, which have been significant drivers of market performance.

The decline in these stocks' moving averages suggests a possible change in investor confidence, as the 50-day moving average is often used as a short-term indicator of a stock's performance. The movement below this threshold could signal a bearish trend, prompting investors to reassess their positions.

Market analysts are closely monitoring these stocks to determine whether this is a temporary fluctuation or the beginning of a more extended downturn. The performance of these mega-cap stocks is crucial, as they have a substantial impact on the broader market indices.

Investors are advised to stay informed about further developments and consider the potential implications for their portfolios. The situation remains dynamic, and market participants are urged to exercise caution in their investment strategies.
